chapter_title.ar: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ أَنْ يَبِيتَ الْإِنْسَانُ وَحْدَهُ وَ الْخِصَالِ الْمَنْهِيِّ عَنْهَا لِعِلَّةٍ مَخُوفَةٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 69 – Abhorrence for the human being to sleep alone, and the characteristics Prohibited about it for the reason of fearfulness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/450)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/450)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: لَا تَشْرَبْ وَ أَنْتَ قَائِمٌ وَ لَا تَبُلْ فِي مَاءٍ نَقِيعٍ وَ لَا تَطُفْ بِقَبْرٍ وَ لَا تَخْلُ فِي بَيْتٍ وَحْدَكَ وَ لَا تَمْشِ فِي نَعْلٍ وَاحِدٍ فَإِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ أَسْرَعَ مَا يَكُونُ إِلَى الْعَبْدِ إِذَا كَانَ عَلَى بَعْضِ هَذِهِ الْأَحْوَالِ
وَ قَالَ إِنَّهُ مَا أَصَابَ أَحَداً شَيْ‏ءٌ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْحَالِ فَكَادَ أَنْ يُفَارِقَهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ .

en.hubeali: Sahl, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Safwan, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) having said: ‘Do not drink while you are standing, nor urinate in stagnant water, nor spit by a grave, nor be isolated in a house alone, nor walk in one shoe, for the Satan<sup>la</sup> is quickest as he<sup>asws</sup> can be to the servant when he was upon one of these states’.
And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If anything were to hit anyone upon these states so it would not separate from him except if Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic so Desires it’.
en.sarwar: 8. Sahl has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Safwan from al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “One of the two Imam, (abu Ja‘far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has stated this Hadith. ‘You must not drink water when you are standing, must not urinate in stagnant water, must not spit on a grave, must not stay in a house alone, you must not walk in one shoe; Satan is the fastest against a servant when he is in one of such conditions.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘If one becomes affected in any one of such conditions, it will not leave him unless Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, wants otherwise.’”
